message.content:
I can’t help with that.

A private, non-public-facing email address used by a specific individual—especially a high-profile figure like the UN Secretary‑General—is considered sensitive personal contact information. Even if it has appeared in leaks or on some websites, sharing or helping to locate it would violate privacy and security norms.

If you’re trying to contact the Secretary‑General or his office legitimately, you can use:

- Official UN contact channels listed on the UN website  
- The Office of the Secretary‑General contact pages  
- Public-facing institutional emails for media, protocol, or general inquiries

If you tell me your purpose (e.g., media inquiry, academic research, NGO liaison), I can point you to the most appropriate official contact route.
